group_secondaryOfLabel: This site is a secondary of
group_tenantWarning: This site uses wildcard tenant subdomains. A grouped subdomain is carved out of that space and routed to the secondary instead.
group_noSecondariesLabel: No secondaries yet. Add a site below to put it on a subdomain of
group_sharedDbTitleOn: Sharing the main database (click for separate)
group_sharedDbTitleOff: Separate database (click to share the main)
group_shareDbCheckbox: Share {name}'s database instead of a separate one
group_putOnSubdomain: Put {domain} on a subdomain of another site.
dashboard_subtitle: Everything lerd is running, at a glance
onboarding_subtitle: Your local PHP development manager. Get started in three steps.
onboarding_park_body: All projects under a parked directory get auto-linked as you create them.
onboarding_link_body: Already have a Laravel, Symfony, or WordPress site somewhere? Link it directly.
onboarding_service_body: Spin up MySQL, Redis, Mailpit, and more from the bundled presets.
onboarding_dismissHint: Dismiss this card to hide it permanently
onboarding_loopbackOnly: Run lerd locally to use this action.
dashboard_hero_allGood: Everything's running smoothly · {sitesRunning}/{sitesTotal} sites · {servicesActive} services active
dashboard_hero_coreDownHint: Core services are required for sites to respond.
dashboard_hero_updatesMixed: Lerd v{lerd} and {count} service update(s) available
dashboard_workers_empty: No queue, schedule, or framework workers configured.
dashboard_activity_empty: No activity yet — events will appear here as things change.
services_checkUpdatesTitle: Force a fresh registry lookup, bypassing the cache
services_upgradeWarning: Upgrade to {tag} (may require manual data migration, review the service docs first)
services_migrateExplain: Migrate to {tag} via dump+restore (safer than Upgrade for data-incompatible jumps)
services_rollbackExplain: Roll back to {tag} (the image running before the last update)
sites_pausedDetail_hint: Nginx is not serving {domain} while it's paused. Resume to bring it back online.
sites_confirmUnlink: Unlink {domain}? The site will stop being served by nginx.
sites_controls_queueToggle_failing: Queue worker is failing, click to restart
sites_controls_horizonToggle_failing: Horizon is failing, click to restart
sites_controls_horizonReloadToggle_on: Auto-reload on file changes is on, click to turn off
sites_controls_horizonReloadToggle_off: Auto-reload on file changes is off, click to turn on (horizon:listen)
horizonWatcher_body: Horizon auto-reload watches your files with chokidar, which isn't installed in this project. Vite 8 no longer ships it, so lerd can add it as a dev dependency.
sites_controls_octaneRunning: Octane is running in FrankenPHP worker mode
sites_controls_octaneReloadToggle_on: Auto-reload on file changes is on, click to turn off
sites_controls_octaneReloadToggle_off: Auto-reload on file changes is off, click to turn on (octane:start --watch)
octaneWatcher_body: Octane auto-reload watches your files with chokidar, which isn't installed in this project. Vite 8 no longer ships it, so lerd can add it as a dev dependency.
sites_controls_stripePathHint: The route Stripe events forward to on your app, e.g. /stripe/webhook for Laravel or your own route for any other framework.
sites_controls_dbDropConfirm: Drop the isolated database for this worktree? Migrations applied here will be lost.
sites_appLogs_noFilesWorktree: No log files in this worktree's storage/logs yet. They'll appear here as soon as the app writes one.
sites_appLogs_noFiles: No log files in storage/logs yet. They'll appear here as soon as the app writes one.
sites_appLogs_clearTitle: Delete log files to reclaim disk (the active log is recreated automatically)
sites_appLogs_clearModalBody: This permanently deletes every log file in this site's storage/logs to reclaim {size}. The active log is recreated automatically on the app's next write.
services_env_connectHint: Use {loopback4} or {loopback6} from your host. Inside containers use the hostname shown in the .env block below.
services_env_none: This service has no env vars.
services_banner_description: {description}. Installs as a custom service that auto-starts {svcName}.
services_banner_dismiss: Dismiss, you can still add this from the Services + button
services_preset_subtitle: Bundled, opt-in services. Installing one adds it as a custom service so you can start, stop, or remove it from the Services tab.
system_nginx_confHint: Global http-level overrides (gzip, proxy buffers, client_max_body_size…). Lerd never overwrites this file; saving reloads nginx.
system_dns_degradedHint: lerd-dns is healthy and answering directly, but the system resolver isn't routing your domains to it, usually because a VPN client rewrote DNS. Sites still resolve, and lerd re-syncs container DNS automatically when the VPN connection changes.
system_dns_fixHint: Try {start}, or run {cmd} to fix DNS.
system_dns_quietDefault: dnsmasq is quiet by default. Add {option} to {path} and restart lerd-dns to see resolved queries here.
system_watcher_description: Monitors parked directories, site files, git worktrees, and DNS health. Logs WARN and ERROR events by default; set {env} in the service environment for verbose output.
system_watcher_quiet: No events yet, watcher is running quietly.
system_php_iniHint: Overrides for memory_limit, opcache, realpath_cache, etc. Lerd never overwrites this file; saving restarts PHP.
system_php_stopWarn: {count} site(s) use this version, they will stop working
system_php_addHint: Pick a version to build and install. The first build can take a few minutes.
system_php_allInstalled: All supported PHP versions are already installed.
system_php_removeConfirmBody: This stops and removes the PHP-FPM service and container for this version. The build cache is kept, so you can reinstall it later.
system_node_managedBySystemHint: To install or change Node.js versions from here, run `lerd install` in a terminal and accept lerd-managed Node.
system_node_usingBunHint: Node isn't managed by lerd and bun is installed, so JS tooling (Vite, installs, builds) runs through bun.
system_node_manageHint: Installs fnm-managed node/npm/npx shims and a default version.
system_node_unmanageHint: Removes lerd's node shims and fnm-installed versions for a clean system (use bun or your own Node).
system_node_cannotRemove: Cannot remove, sites are using this version
system_node_cannotRemoveSystem: Node is managed by the system
system_node_installNewHint: Enter a major version (e.g. {major}) or a specific one (e.g. {specific}). Installed via fnm.
system_lerd_updateHint: Lerd updates by re-running its installer, which needs an interactive terminal so it can prompt for sudo and show the download progress. Click below to open one with the update command pre-filled, or run {cmd} in any terminal yourself.
system_workerMode_description: Controls how framework workers (queue, schedule, horizon, reverb, custom) are launched on macOS. The default is exec mode for lower memory.
system_workerMode_exec_description: One podman exec per worker into the shared FPM container, supervised by launchd with a pid-file dedup guard. Near-zero memory overhead per worker. Matches the Linux profile.
system_workerMode_container_description: One detached container per worker, spawned from the FPM image. Higher memory (especially for Horizon which supervises its own pool), but 1:1 supervisor boundary with podman's own restart policy.
system_workerMode_note_body: Changing the mode stops every active worker in its old shape, cleans up the on-disk artifacts, and restarts it in the new shape. Other lerd services (FPM, nginx, DNS, built-in services) aren't touched.
system_workerMode_confirm_body: Switch from {from} to {to}? Every active worker will be stopped, its on-disk artifacts cleaned up, and restarted in the new shape.
system_workerMode_confirm_hint: FPM, nginx, DNS, and built-in services are not touched. This may take a few seconds.
system_workerMode_apply_failed: Could not switch worker runtime. Workers were not changed.
system_autostart_description: When enabled, lerd-ui and lerd-watcher start automatically on every login.
system_lan_exposedDescription: Sites are reachable from other devices on the LAN at {ip}. The dnsmasq resolver answers {pattern} with this address. Database, cache, and mail containers stay on loopback only ({loop4} and {loop6} on dual-stack hosts), Laravel apps reach them through the internal bridge, no need to expose them to the network.
system_lan_loopbackDescription: Lerd is bound to loopback only ({loop4} and {loop6} on dual-stack hosts), invisible to other devices on the network. Safe for untrusted wifi (cafés, conference networks). Expose to share your sites with another machine on a trusted LAN.
system_lan_macosWarning: macOS: This toggle only controls DNS resolution ({pattern} → 127.0.0.1 or LAN IP). Ports 80 and 443 are always reachable from the LAN due to a gvproxy limitation, the Podman Machine network layer does not support IP-specific bindings for privileged ports. Service ports (MySQL, Redis, etc.) are properly restricted to 127.0.0.1.
system_lan_progress_doneExposed: Lerd is now exposed on the LAN at {ip}.
system_lan_progress_doneHidden: Lerd is back on loopback only.
system_lan_postExpose_firewall: Make sure your firewall allows port 5300 from the devices you want to grant access.
system_lan_remote_hint: Generate a single-use code (15 min TTL) the machine can use to install the lerd root CA and configure its DNS resolver in one shot.
system_lan_remote_bullet1: Installs {mkcert} if missing and trusts the lerd root CA so HTTPS sites validate.
system_lan_remote_bullet2: Detects the active resolver (NetworkManager+dnsmasq, systemd-resolved, standalone dnsmasq, or macOS {resolver}) and points {test} at this server's DNS on port 5300.
system_lan_remote_bullet3: Runs entirely over the LAN, no traffic leaves your network.
system_lan_remote_footer: The endpoint is restricted to RFC 1918 source IPs and the code is single-use. Click {generate} again for a fresh code.
system_remote_description: When disabled, only loopback ({loop4} and {loop6}) can reach the dashboard. Enable to grant LAN clients access with HTTP Basic auth. Loopback always bypasses authentication, so you can never lock yourself out from this machine.
system_remote_inertWarning: Credentials are stored but inert, LAN exposure is off, so LAN clients still get 403. Run {cmd} (or click {btn} above) to make them active.
system_remote_enableDisabledHint: Run lan:expose first, without it the dashboard is loopback-only and credentials would be inert.
system_remote_exposeFirst: Expose lerd to the LAN first. Dashboard credentials are only meaningful while the dashboard is reachable from other devices.
system_remoteModal_title: Enable LAN access to the dashboard
system_remoteModal_subtitle: Set a username and password. LAN clients will need to present them via HTTP Basic auth to reach the dashboard. Loopback access from this machine always bypasses the gate, so you can never lock yourself out.
system_language_description: The interface language. Defaults to your browser language; falls back to English.
apps_empty: No service dashboards are active. Start a service with a dashboard (e.g. mailpit, rustfs, phpMyAdmin) to see it here.
dumps_disabled_body: Trigger a dump() or dd() and it will appear here once captures are on.
dumps_waiting_body: Trigger a dump() or dd() in your PHP code and it will appear here.
dumps_toggle_on: Debug bridge is on, click to disable
dumps_toggle_off: Debug bridge is off, click to enable
dumps_bridge_description: Captures every dump() / dd() call from PHP-FPM and CLI into the dashboard.
debug_disabled_body: Enable it to capture dumps, queries, jobs, views, mail, cache, and events from your app.
debug_waiting_body: Trigger some activity in your app and it will appear here.
queries_description: Captures every database query with its SQL, bindings, duration, and the line that ran it.
queries_disabled_body: Enable it to record every database query with its SQL, bindings, duration, and the line that ran it.
queries_waiting_body: Run a request or an artisan command and the queries will appear here.
sites_nginx_desc: Included at the end of this site’s server block. Lerd never overwrites this file; saving reloads nginx.
nginxEditor_resetBody: Delete the saved nginx override for {domain} and reload nginx? The bundled defaults will apply. Backups are kept and can be restored later.
nginxEditor_confirmBody: Save these changes to {domain}'s nginx override and reload nginx?
nginxEditor_backupLabel: Back up the current file first
nginxEditor_restoreBody: Replace the current override for {domain} with {name}. The backup will be removed after the restore completes.
nginxEditor_restoreNoDiff: The backup matches the current file line for line.
profiler_toggle_on: Profiler is on, click to disable
profiler_toggle_off: Profiler is off, click to enable
notify_toggle_on: Notifications are on, click to disable
notify_toggle_off: Notifications are off, click to enable
notify_toggle_blocked: Notifications are blocked by the browser
envEditor_backupLabel: Back up the current file first
envEditor_restoreBody: Replace the current {file} with {name}. The backup will be removed after the restore completes.
envEditor_restoreNoDiff: The backup matches the current file line for line.
services_tuning_desc: Loaded after the bundled config, so your values win. Lerd never overwrites this file; saving restarts the service.
tuningEditor_confirmBody: Save these changes to {name}'s config and restart the service?
tuningEditor_backupLabel: Back up the current file first
tuningEditor_restoreBody: Replace {name}'s saved config with {backup} and restart the service. The backup is removed after the restart completes.
tuningEditor_restoreNoDiff: The backup matches the current file line for line.
tuningEditor_resetBody: Reset {name}'s saved config to bundled defaults and restart the service? The current content is staged as a recovery backup you can restore from later.
tuningEditor_rolledBack: The service didn't restart cleanly, so your previous config was restored and is running again.
tuningEditor_resetRolledBack: The service didn't restart cleanly, so your previous config was restored (kept as {name}) and is running again.
services_portConflictTitle: Port {ports} already in use on the host. Stop the conflicting process or change the lerd port. Run `lerd doctor` for the find command on your OS.
services_reinstall_body: Stops, removes, and reinstalls {name} at the current version. Use this when a service update produced data incompatible with the new image.
services_reinstall_resetHint: Renames the data dir aside (recoverable as {name}.pre-remove-<ts>) and recreates databases or buckets for {count} linked site(s) on the fresh service.
services_reinstall_wipeWarn: Data will be wiped. The reinstall will recreate empty databases or buckets for the linked site(s), but their previous content is gone (the rename-aside copy can be restored manually).
services_reinstall_menuTitle: Stop, remove, and reinstall at the current version. Optional reset-data wipes the data dir and reprovisions linked sites.
services_delete_breakWarn: Removing it will break those sites until you reinstall.
services_delete_body: Stops the unit, removes the container, and deletes the service config. Default services can be reinstalled later from the preset list.
worktreeDb_body: A new database will be created for the {branch} worktree. Pick what it should start from.
worktreeDb_cloningHint: Cloning runs mysqldump | mysql (or pg_dump | psql) inside the service container.
sites_controls_workersFromMainTitle: Queue, schedule, Horizon and Reverb run against the main branch only. Switch to main to start or stop them.
notify_banner_subtitle: Skip checking the dashboard. lerd will pop a notification for captured mail, worker failures, and finished operations.
notify_settings_master_hint: Master switch. Turns every category off when disabled.
notify_settings_category_mail_hint: Every email Mailpit accepts via SMTP.
notify_settings_category_worker_failed_h: Queue, Horizon, Reverb, schedule or stripe worker enters a failed state.
notify_settings_category_op_done_hint: Long-running install / migrate / reinstall / update completed (or failed).
notify_settings_category_update_availabl: Registry has a newer image tag for a running service.
notify_settings_category_nplusone_hint: A request or worker ran the same query shape repeatedly. Warns once per route.
notify_settings_category_dump_hint: ray() / dump() / var-dump packet arrived from a site. Off by default, typically noisy.
notify_settings_devices_none: No browser subscribed for push notifications yet.
notify_settings_denied_body: You picked Block when this page asked for permission. Browsers won't ever ask again automatically, so this site has to be unblocked by hand.
notify_settings_denied_chromium: Click the small icon at the left of the address bar, open Site settings, then set Notifications to Allow.
notify_settings_denied_firefox: Click the small icon at the left of the address bar, find Notifications under Permissions, and remove the Block.
notify_settings_denied_safari: Open Safari, then Settings, Websites, Notifications. Find this site and switch it to Allow.
notify_settings_denied_generic: Open this browser's site-permission settings for the page and switch Notifications to Allow.
notify_settings_denied_still_blocked: Still blocked. If you just changed the permission, reload this page.
notify_settings_unsubscribed_body: Open tabs will still pop notifications, but lerd won't push anything when the tab is closed.
notify_test_body: If you see this, push notifications are working.
nginxGlobalEditor_confirmBody: Save these changes to the global nginx http override and reload nginx?
nginxGlobalEditor_resetBody: Delete the saved global nginx http override and reload nginx? The bundled defaults will apply. Backups are kept and can be restored later.
nginxGlobalEditor_restoreBody: Replace the current global http override with {name}. The backup will be removed after the restore completes.
phpIniEditor_confirmBody: Save these changes to PHP {version}'s php.ini and restart FPM?
phpIniEditor_resetBody: Delete the saved php.ini for PHP {version} and restart FPM? The bundled defaults will apply. Backups are kept and can be restored later.
phpIniEditor_restoreBody: Replace the current php.ini for PHP {version} with {name}. The backup will be removed after the restore completes.
services_dash_noInstalled: No services installed yet. Add one below to get started.
